title: Looking Back over Shoulder
description: A classic elegant pose looking back over the shoulder. Great for outdoor and street photography.
instructions: Stand straight with feet shoulder-width apart
Shift your weight to one leg slightly
Slowly turn your upper body about 45 degrees away from camera
Look back over your shoulder toward the camera
Keep your chin slightly down for a flattering angle
Relax your shoulders and breathe naturally
Hold the pose and smile softly

title: Casual Walk
description: Walking casually towards the camera with confidence. Perfect for street and lifestyle shots.
instructions: Start about 5 meters from the camera
Begin walking toward the camera at a slow, relaxed pace
Keep your hands in your pockets or swing them naturally
Look straight at the camera or slightly past it
Keep your chin level and shoulders relaxed
Let your stride be natural — not too big, not too small
Stop when you reach the gold outline position

title: Romantic Embrace
description: A close romantic hug from the front. Perfect for couples and anniversary shoots.
instructions: Stand facing each other, very close together
The taller person wraps both arms around the other
The shorter person places hands on the other's chest or sides
Both lean in slightly, foreheads almost touching
Close your eyes or look at each other naturally
Smile gently — be in the moment
Hold the pose until the shutter fires

title: Jump Together
description: Friends jumping together in the air for an energetic, fun group photo.
instructions: All friends stand shoulder-to-shoulder in a line
Set a countdown — 3, 2, 1, jump!
Everyone jumps at the same time as high as possible
Raise arms or spread them out for more energy
Smile and laugh — the joy shows in the photo
The photographer uses burst mode to catch the peak jump
Retry 2-3 times until the timing is perfect

title: Golden Hour Wanderer
description: A dreamy solo travel pose for outdoor golden hour photography.
instructions: Find a scenic outdoor spot with open space behind you
Stand with one foot slightly in front of the other
Place one hand near your face, touching your hair lightly
Let the other arm hang naturally at your side
Tilt your head slightly toward the raised hand
Look into the distance or gently toward the camera
Breathe out slowly for a natural, relaxed expression
